daily_fact: Curbed highlighted a rare Prospect–Lefferts Gardens brownstone featuring its own backyard swimming pool, showcasing one of Brooklyn’s most distinctive residential amenities.
topline_md: New York City faces a stormy, disruption-heavy weekend as more severe weather and parade-related road closures hit while officials juggle major policy fights over the city budget, schools, housing costs, and immigration enforcement.

items.title: Weekend storms could bring hazardous weather to NYC
items.why_it_matters: Storms may disrupt travel and outdoor plans across the city before conditions improve.

items.title: Parades and festivals will close NYC roads this weekend
items.why_it_matters: Street closures can delay driving, buses, and local deliveries in multiple neighborhoods.

items.title: City Council presses priorities in NYC budget talks
items.why_it_matters: Budget decisions shape school funding, housing, safety, and services residents rely on every day.

items.title: NYC plans smaller class sizes for 2026-27 school year
items.why_it_matters: Smaller classes could improve learning conditions for students and staffing needs across city schools.

items.title: Hochul says NYC preparing for possible ICE surge
items.why_it_matters: A federal immigration enforcement increase could affect families, schools, workplaces, and city agencies.

items.title: Brooklyn Third Avenue safety redesign stalls amid truck traffic
items.why_it_matters: Delays to a street safety overhaul leave pedestrians and cyclists facing ongoing crash risks.

items.title: Council weighs competing plans for Central Park carriage horses
items.why_it_matters: The decision could change a major tourist industry, animal welfare rules, and nearby jobs.

items.title: Broker fee ban enforcement remains limited a year later
items.why_it_matters: Weak enforcement can keep apartment hunters paying costs the city intended to eliminate.

items.title: State offers $10 million for food banks and emergency food aid
items.why_it_matters: New funding could expand meals and grocery support for New Yorkers facing food insecurity.

items.title: Housing lottery opens at 282 4th Avenue in Gowanus
items.why_it_matters: The lottery creates a new chance for eligible residents to secure below-market housing in Brooklyn.

items.title: Students urge NYC to restore school hearing screenings
items.why_it_matters: Resuming screenings could help students with hearing loss get earlier support and classroom accommodations.

items.title: NYC Public Schools plans new Bronx STEAM center
items.why_it_matters: The project could expand science and technology learning opportunities for Bronx students.

items.title: Manhattan rents hit another record in May
items.why_it_matters: Record rents directly affect housing costs, lease renewals, and apartment searches for Manhattan residents.

items.title: Chinese immigrants become NYC’s largest foreign-born group
items.why_it_matters: The shift reflects changing neighborhoods, service needs, and political representation across the city.
